assertions.text: Another car pulled out in front of the narrator on the fast road.
The narrator was traveling at around 110 km/h before braking.
The narrator performed an emergency braking maneuver.
The narrator's speed reduced from around 110 km/h down to walking pace within a few seconds.
There was no contact between vehicles (zero impacts).
After coming to walking pace and clearing the situation, the narrator accelerated and picked up speed again.
The emergency braking occurred after the other car pulled out in front of the narrator.
The collision severity was none/avoided (no collision occurred).

verdicts.evidence: The presence of another vehicle pulling out is not directly observable in the vehicle's sensors; GPS speed shows changes but does not identify other road users. Sensor data shows GPS speed samples such as [113.0 km/h at t=0.0](T4) and deceleration events [drop from 113.0 to 78.0 km/h between t=0.0 and t=3.0](T3) but that does not prove the cause was another car.
GPS speed at the start of the window is [113.0 km/h at t=0.0](T4), which supports the claim of traveling around [110 km/h](T4) before braking.

A sustained deceleration was detected on an accelerometer axis with mean magnitude [0.55 g between t=0.22 and t=4.11](T3), and multiple GPS speed drops meet the rapid-drop criterion including [113.0 to 78.0 km/h from t=0.0 to t=3.0](T3), consistent with an emergency braking maneuver.

GPS speed reduced from about [113.0 km/h at t=0.0] to [10.0 km/h at t=8.0] with intermediate samples [55.0 km/h at t=4.0] and [26.0 km/h at t=6.0] (T4), and integrated device-frame delta-V on accel_x is [−51.8 km/h over the 0–16 s window](T2), supporting a rapid reduction from ~[110 km/h](T4) to around walking pace within a few seconds.

The detect_events output shows no impact spike or CISS impact detection (no crash-pulse event) and GPS speed samples show recovery without a collision signature; accel peaks are below high-impact thresholds (peak accel on accel_x is [−0.777 g at t=1.12](T2)), consistent with no contact recorded.

After slowing to low speeds (GPS shows [10.0 km/h at t=8.0] and [9.0 km/h at t=9.0](T4)), the vehicle later increased speed to [42.0 km/h at t=15.0](T4), indicating the narrator accelerated and picked up speed again.

Sensor timing shows deceleration begins early (sustained accel_x event starts at [t=0.22](T3)) but sensors do not identify another vehicle or the relative timing of that vehicle pulling out; therefore whether braking occurred after another car pulled out is unverifiable from the telemetry.

There is no crash-pulse/impact detection in the event summary and the accelerometer peak ([−0.777 g at t=1.12](T2)) is below impact thresholds; GPS speed shows no abrupt stop due to collision and the vehicle resumes driving, supporting that no collision occurred (severity none/avoided).

result.rationale: Telemetry shows an initial high speed ([113.0 km/h](T4)), rapid sustained deceleration ([0.55 g between t=0.22–4.11](T3)), no detected impact/crash pulse ([no impact detections in T3], peak accel [−0.777 g at t=1.12](T2)), and later speed recovery to [42.0 km/h at t=15.0](T4), supporting a high-speed emergency braking event with no collision; however, presence and timing of another vehicle pulling out are not observable from these sensors.

citations.claim: drop from 113.0 to 78.0 km/h between t=0.0 and t=3.0
113.0 to 78.0 km/h from t=0.0 to t=3.0

result.notes: 100 Hz IMU over a 16 s window. Units: accel g, gyro deg/s, speed km/h. Device-to-vehicle axis alignment is NOT documented; do not assume which axis is longitudinal without cross-checking against GPS speed.

accel_x.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_y.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

accel_z.integrated_delta_v_note: integral of (a - baseline) over the range; device-frame axis, sign/orientation not vehicle-aligned

detections.note: direction ambiguous: device axis orientation undocumented

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|

result.downsampling: per-bucket sample with the largest |deviation from channel median| is kept
